story.carpenter_e1.e1_scene1.text:
...Done.
What a nightmare customer.
So problem clients exist in this world too, huh.
story.carpenter_e1.e1_scene2.text:
The townspeople are stirring as they look at me.
What, do they want to thank me?
...This is embarrassing. I just did my job.
story.carpenter_e1.e1_scene3.text:
I heard about it at the tavern.
Apparently there's a corrupt contractor running this town.
A man who cuts corners and tramples craftsmen underfoot.
story.carpenter_e1.e1_scene4.text:
As a craftsman, I can't let that slide.
My job is to see things through to completion.
Let's move to the next site.
story.carpenter_e2.e2_scene1.text:
...It's over.
A man who exploited and trampled craftsmen with money and power.
There's always one of these guys in any world.
story.carpenter_e2.e2_scene2.text:
The exploited craftsmen surrounded me.
They're saying I freed them.
...I just did my job. That's all.
story.carpenter_e2.e2_scene3.text:
Then it hit me.
That World Tree where I first woke up.
Something's there. Something that called me to this place.
story.carpenter_e2.e2_scene4.text:
I've never walked away from any site.
I'm heading to that World Tree.
This is my final job.
story.carpenter_e3.e3_scene1.text:
So you're the one who called me here.
Why me?
Answer me.
story.carpenter_e3.e3_scene2.text:
The guardian spoke quietly.
This world is falling apart, it said.
It called someone who could rebuild it.
story.carpenter_e3.e3_scene3.text:
...So I'm the one meant to rebuild this world.
That's funny. A carpenter saving the world.
But my job is still to see things through to completion.
story.carpenter_e3.e3_scene4.text:
The guardian said it would open a path back to my world.
I thought for a moment.
...No. I still have work to do here.
story.carpenter_e3.e3_scene5.text:
This world has sites that need my skills.
My old world can wait.
I'm still a carpenter.
story.carpenter_opening.scene1.text:
...Where am I?
I was working on site when the scaffolding collapsed...
When I came to, I was lying at the base of a massive tree.
story.carpenter_opening.scene2.text:
This tree... it's too big. I've never seen anything like it.
No phone signal. No signal at all.
This isn't the world I know.
story.carpenter_opening.scene3.text:
...Well, it doesn't matter where I am.
A carpenter builds things on any site.
With tools and skill, you survive.
story.carpenter_opening.scene4.text:
I can see a town in the distance. Let me head there first.
...Anyone who gets in my way will feel my hammer.
Craftsman's work is done carefully. And done right.
story.carpenter_opening.scene5.text:
Whether it's another world or not, the job doesn't change.
My work is to see things through to completion.
Let's go.
story.cook_e1.e1_scene1.text:
...That's settled.
Deceiving people with rotten ingredients isn't even cooking—it's a disgrace.
Don't mock the craft.
story.cook_e1.e1_scene2.text:
The townspeople gathered around.
Are you hungry? ...Yeah, I figured.
You all have the look of people who haven't eaten properly.
story.cook_e1.e1_scene3.text:
I made a pot with whatever scraps I could find.
They're eating it, some of them crying it's so good.
...Yeah, cooking really is something special.
story.cook_e1.e1_scene4.text:
Apparently there's someone controlling all the food in this town.
A head chef who poisons people to make them obey.
...I can't stand for that. Let's go.
story.cook_e2.e2_scene1.text:
I defeated the poisoner chef.
Using food to hurt people—that's backwards.
A knife is meant to give life, not take it.
story.cook_e2.e2_scene2.text:
The freed people are offering me ingredients.
They want me to cook for them.
...Yeah, leave it to me. I'll make enough for everyone.
story.cook_e2.e2_scene3.text:
That giant tree caught my eye.
There's something at its roots. I can feel it calling to me.
...Maybe the final ingredient is waiting there.
story.cook_e2.e2_scene4.text:
I've stood in every kind of kitchen. I know tough spots.
I'm heading for that tree.
This is my last course.
story.cook_e3.e3_scene1.text:
So you called for me.
What does a cook like me have to offer?
...Are you hungry?
story.cook_e3.e3_scene2.text:
The guardian spoke quietly.
This world is starving, they said.
In body and spirit alike.
story.cook_e3.e3_scene3.text:
...That's why you called a cook, then.
My one dish will save the world? That's ambitious.
But I like it. That order.
story.cook_e3.e3_scene4.text:
The guardian said I could return to my world.
I hesitated for a moment.
...No, I didn't. I already knew the answer.
story.cook_e3.e3_scene5.text:
There are still people starving in this world.
My job isn't done until everyone's been fed.
That's what being a cook means.
story.cook_opening.scene1.text:
...It's hot. Where am I?
There was a fire in the kitchen, I think... I got the customers out...
When I came to, I was in an unfamiliar forest.
story.cook_opening.scene2.text:
The sky looks different. The air smells different.
But... on this breeze, I smell it.
Someone, somewhere, is cooking.
story.cook_opening.scene3.text:
My knife calluses are still here. These hands remember.
How to handle fire, how to balance flavors.
A chef's skills don't rust, even when the world changes.
story.cook_opening.scene4.text:
You can't fight on an empty stomach, as they say.
First, I'll search that town for ingredients.
...Anyone who gets in my way gets flambéed.
story.cook_opening.scene5.text:
Whether it's another world or not, it doesn't matter.
If there's someone hungry, I'll cook for them.
Let's find a kitchen.
victory.title: Clear!
zukan.title: Compendium
